DEBATE: Step 1.3 AppShell Component — TUI Rewrite (React/Ink)

You are implementing Step 1.3 from a TDD rewrite plan. Your job is to produce:
  1. The test file: src/tui/app-shell.test.tsx
  2. The component file: src/tui/app-shell.tsx

CONTEXT — What already exists:
  - src/tui/header.tsx: exports Header (React.memo FC) and HEADER_LINES = 3
    Props: { currentView: View, columns: number, activeAgents?, maxAgents?, unreadCount?, threadInfo? }
  - src/tui/footer.tsx: exports Footer (React.memo FC), FooterOptions, computeFooterLines, getFooterShortcuts
    FooterProps: { viewType: ViewType, inThread?, responseSelected?, hasReplies?, isQuoting?, focusedAction?, inputFocused?, threadResolved?, focusedIndex?, columns? }
    FooterOptions: { inThread?, responseSelected?, hasReplies?, isQuoting?, focusedAction?, inputFocused? }
  - src/tui/views.ts: ViewType enum (Home, Detail, NewIssue, AgentStatus, BlockingMap, GroupView, IssuePicker), View union type
  - Tests use vitest + ink-testing-library. Pattern: import { render } from 'ink-testing-library'; const { lastFrame } = render(<Component />);
  - Tests also use: import { renderToString } from 'ink'; and stripAnsi from 'strip-ansi'.
  - ESM project: imports use .js extensions (e.g., './views.js')

REQUIREMENTS for AppShell:

  1. AppShell is a NEW functional component in src/tui/app-shell.tsx
  2. It provides the 3-zone layout: Header (top), content area (middle), Footer (bottom)
  3. Props:
     - columns: number
     - rows: number
     - currentView: View (in Phase 1; Phase 2 will read from NavigationContext instead)
     - activeAgents?: number
     - maxAgents?: number
     - unreadCount?: number
     - threadInfo?: { inThread: boolean }
     - children: (setFooterOptions: (opts: FooterOptions) => void) => React.ReactNode
       (render prop pattern — children is a function that receives setFooterOptions)
  4. AppShell owns footerOptions state: useState<FooterOptions>({})
  5. AppShell passes setFooterOptions to children via the render prop
  6. Layout:
     <Box flexDirection="column" height={rows}>
       <Header currentView={currentView} columns={columns} activeAgents={...} ... />
       <Box flexDirection="column" flexGrow={1}>
         {children(setFooterOptions)}
       </Box>
       <Footer viewType={currentView.type} {...footerOptions} columns={columns} />
     </Box>
  7. The content area gets remaining height = rows - HEADER_LINES - footerLines
     where footerLines = computeFooterLines(getFooterShortcuts(viewType, footerOptions), columns)
  8. Export the component and any types needed

TESTS to write (Step 1.3 gate tests — all must pass):

  test: "app shell renders header, content, footer vertically"
    - Render AppShell with children that return <Text>CONTENT</Text>
    - Verify output contains the header title, "CONTENT", and footer shortcuts

  test: "app shell constrains height to rows prop"
    - Render AppShell with rows=24
    - Verify the outer Box has height={24}

  test: "content area gets remaining height after header and footer"
    - Render AppShell, check that content area height = rows - HEADER_LINES - footerLines
    - Use computeFooterLines to calculate expected footer height

  test: "resize updates dimensions" (Note: this may be tested at run.tsx level)
    - If testable at AppShell level: re-render with different columns/rows, verify layout updates

  test: "setFooterOptions state reaches Footer props"
    - Render AppShell with children that call setFooterOptions({ inThread: true })
    - Verify Footer receives inThread=true (check that thread shortcuts appear)

CONSTRAINTS:
  - TDD: Write tests first, then implement
  - Functional component only (no class components)
  - React.memo is optional for AppShell (it re-renders when state changes)
  - Use ESM imports with .js extensions
  - Do NOT use ANSI escape codes for clearing screen
  - Do NOT import mock data — AppShell is data-agnostic
  - Keep it minimal — no navigation logic (that's Phase 2)

DELIVERABLES:
  Provide the complete contents of:
  1. src/tui/app-shell.test.tsx — all 5 tests
  2. src/tui/app-shell.tsx — the component implementation

  Make the tests pass. Explain any design decisions.

mcp startup: no servers

import React, { memo } from 'react';
import { Box, Text } from 'ink';
import { type View, ViewType, ViewTypeStringsMap } from './views.js';

    currentView: View;
    columns: number;
    activeAgents?: number;
    maxAgents?: number;
    unreadCount?: number;
    threadInfo?: { inThread: boolean };
}

/// <reference types="node" resolution-mode="require"/>
import { EventEmitter } from 'node:events';
import type { ReactElement } from 'react';
declare class Stdout extends EventEmitter {
    get columns(): number;
    readonly frames: string[];
    private _lastFrame?;
    write: (frame: string) => void;
    lastFrame: () => string | undefined;
}
declare class Stderr extends EventEmitter {
    readonly frames: string[];
    private _lastFrame?;
    write: (frame: string) => void;
    lastFrame: () => string | undefined;
}
declare class Stdin extends EventEmitter {
    isTTY: boolean;
    data: string | null;
    constructor(options?: {
 succeeded in 51ms:
import { EventEmitter } from 'node:events';
import { render as inkRender } from 'ink';
class Stdout extends EventEmitter {
    get columns() {
        return 100;
    }
    frames = [];
    _lastFrame;
    write = (frame) => {
        this.frames.push(frame);
        this._lastFrame = frame;
    };
    lastFrame = () => this._lastFrame;
}
class Stderr extends EventEmitter {
    frames = [];
    _lastFrame;
    write = (frame) => {
        this.frames.push(frame);
        this._lastFrame = frame;

import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import React from 'react';
import { render } from 'ink-testing-library';
import { HomeView } from './home-view.js';
import type { Issue } from '../types.js';
import { IssueStatus } from "../types.js";

const tick = () => new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 0));

function makeIssue(overrides: Partial<Issue> & { inum: number; title: string }): Issue {
    return {
        description: '',
        status: IssueStatus.Awaiting,
        created_at: '2026-01-01T00:00:00Z',
        resolved_at: null,
        issue_revision: 1,
        agent_last_read_at: null,
        user_last_viewed_at: null,
        ...overrides,
    };

src/tui/detail.test.tsx:16:vi.mock('./mock-store.js', () => ({
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,120p' src/tui/detail.test.tsx" in /Users/matkatmusicllc/Programming/authv5-workspace/review-agent-orchestrator succeeded in 52ms:
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import React from 'react';
import { render } from 'ink-testing-library';
import { DetailView } from './detail.js';
import { ResponseContainer } from './response-container.js';
import type { Issue, Response, Message } from '../types.js';
import { IssueStatus, ResponseType, AuthorType } from '../types.js';
import { createMessage, buildResponseChain, buildMixedChain } from './thread-builders.js';

let mockNextId = 100;
vi.mock('./mock-data.js', () => ({
    getMockStore: () => ({ nextResponseId: mockNextId }),
    saveMockStore: vi.fn(),
    MOCK_DETAIL_DATA: {} as Record<number, unknown>,
}));
vi.mock('./mock-store.js', () => ({
    getNextResponseId: (store: { nextResponseId: number }) => {
        const id = store.nextResponseId;
        store.nextResponseId = id + 1;
        mockNextId = store.nextResponseId;
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "rg -n \"measureElement|measure\" src node_modules/ink/build --glob '"'!node_modules/**/README*'"'" in /Users/matkatmusicllc/Programming/authv5-workspace/review-agent-orchestrator succeeded in 52ms:
node_modules/ink/build/measure-element.js:4:const measureElement = (node) => ({
node_modules/ink/build/measure-element.js:8:export default measureElement;
node_modules/ink/build/measure-element.js:9://# sourceMappingURL=measure-element.js.map
node_modules/ink/build/measure-text.js:3:const measureText = (text) => {
node_modules/ink/build/measure-text.js:20:export default measureText;
node_modules/ink/build/measure-text.js:21://# sourceMappingURL=measure-text.js.map
src/tui/app-shell.test.tsx:65:        // Render with a child that fills its area so we can measure
node_modules/ink/build/measure-text.d.ts:5:declare const measureText: (text: string) => Output;
node_modules/ink/build/measure-text.d.ts:6:export default measureText;
node_modules/ink/build/experimental/dom.js:10:var _measureText = _interopRequireDefault(require("../measure-text"));
node_modules/ink/build/experimental/dom.js:107:    const dimensions = (0, _measureText.default)(text);
node_modules/ink/build/calculate-wrapped-text.js:8:var _measureText = _interopRequireDefault(require("./measure-text"));
node_modules/ink/build/calculate-wrapped-text.js:37:      } = (0, _measureText.default)(wrappedText);
node_modules/ink/build/dom.js:2:import measureText from './measure-text.js';
node_modules/ink/build/dom.js:17:        node.yogaNode?.setMeasureFunc(measureTextNode.bind(null, node));
node_modules/ink/build/dom.js:90:const measureTextNode = function (node, width) {
node_modules/ink/build/dom.js:92:    const dimensions = measureText(text);
node_modules/ink/build/dom.js:104:    return measureText(wrappedText);
node_modules/ink/build/dom.js:113:    // Mark closest Yoga node as dirty to measure text dimensions again
node_modules/ink/build/index.d.ts:31:export { default as measureElement } from './measure-element.js';
exec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' node_modules/ink/build/components/Box.d.ts" in /Users/matkatmusicllc/Programming/authv5-workspace/review-agent-orchestratorexec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,200p' node_modules/ink/build/measure-element.d.ts" in /Users/matkatmusicllc/Programming/authv5-workspace/review-agent-orchestratorexec
/bin/zsh -lc "sed -n '1,220p' node_modules/ink/build/dom.d.ts" in /Users/matkatmusicllc/Programming/authv5-workspace/review-agent-orchestrator succeeded in 52ms:
import { type Node as YogaNode } from 'yoga-layout';
import { type Styles } from './styles.js';
import { type OutputTransformer } from './render-node-to-output.js';
type InkNode = {
    parentNode: DOMElement | undefined;
    yogaNode?: YogaNode;
    internal_static?: boolean;
    style: Styles;
};
export type TextName = '#text';
export type ElementNames = 'ink-root' | 'ink-box' | 'ink-text' | 'ink-virtual-text';
export type NodeNames = ElementNames | TextName;
export type DOMElement = {
    nodeName: ElementNames;
    attributes: Record<string, DOMNodeAttribute>;
    childNodes: DOMNode[];
    internal_transform?: OutputTransformer;
    internal_accessibility?: {
        role?: 'button' | 'checkbox' | 'combobox' | 'list' | 'listbox' | 'listitem' | 'menu' | 'menuitem' | 'option' | 'progressbar' | 'radio' | 'radiogroup' | 'tab' | 'tablist' | 'table' | 'textbox' | 'timer' | 'toolbar';
        state?: {
 succeeded in 52ms:
import { type DOMElement } from './dom.js';
type Output = {
    /**
    Element width.
    */
    width: number;
    /**
    Element height.
    */
    height: number;
};
/**
Measure the dimensions of a particular `<Box>` element.
*/
declare const measureElement: (node: DOMElement) => Output;
export default measureElement;
 succeeded in 52ms:
import React from 'react';
import { type Except } from 'type-fest';
import { type Styles } from '../styles.js';
import { type DOMElement } from '../dom.js';
export type Props = Except<Styles, 'textWrap'> & {
    /**
    A label for the element for screen readers.
    */
    readonly 'aria-label'?: string;
    /**
    Hide the element from screen readers.
    */
    readonly 'aria-hidden'?: boolean;
    /**
    The role of the element.
    */
    readonly 'aria-role'?: 'button' | 'checkbox' | 'combobox' | 'list' | 'listbox' | 'listitem' | 'menu' | 'menuitem' | 'option' | 'progressbar' | 'radio' | 'radiogroup' | 'tab' | 'tablist' | 'table' | 'textbox' | 'timer' | 'toolbar';
    /**
    The state of the element.
    */
codex
The workspace is read-only here, so I couldn’t write or run these files directly. These are the complete contents.

`src/tui/app-shell.test.tsx`
```tsx
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import React, { useEffect } from 'react';
import { render } from 'ink-testing-library';
import { renderToString, Text } from 'ink';
import stripAnsi from 'strip-ansi';
import { AppShell as RealAppShell } from './app-shell.js';
import { HEADER_LINES } from './header.js';
import {
    computeFooterLines,
    getFooterShortcuts,
} from './footer.js';
import type { FooterOptions, FooterProps } from './footer.js';
import { ViewType } from './views.js';

const tick = () => new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 0));
const homeView = { type: ViewType.Home as const };

async function loadInstrumentedAppShell() {
    vi.resetModules();

    const boxCalls: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];

    vi.doMock('ink', async () => {
        const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('ink')>('ink');

        return {
            ...actual,
            Box: ({ children, ...props }: React.PropsWithChildren<Record<string, unknown>>) => {
                boxCalls.push(props);
                return <>{children}</>;
            },
        };
    });

    vi.doMock('./header.js', async () => {
        const { Text } = await vi.importActual<typeof import('ink')>('ink');

        return {
            HEADER_LINES,
            Header: () => <Text>HEADER</Text>,
        };
    });

    vi.doMock('./footer.js', async () => {
        const { Text } = await vi.importActual<typeof import('ink')>('ink');

        return {
            Footer: (props: FooterProps) => (
                <Text>{props.inThread ? 'THREAD FOOTER' : 'FOOTER'}</Text>
            ),
            computeFooterLines,
            getFooterShortcuts,
        };
    });

    const { AppShell } = await import('./app-shell.js');
    return { AppShell, boxCalls };
}

describe('AppShell', () => {
    it('app shell renders header, content, footer vertically', () => {
        const columns = 120;
        const rows = 12;
        const output = stripAnsi(renderToString(
            <RealAppShell columns={columns} rows={rows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>CONTENT</Text>}
            </RealAppShell>,
            { columns },
        ));

        const lines = output.split('\n');
        const headerIndex = lines.findIndex(line => line.includes('Review Agent Orchestrator'));
        const contentIndex = lines.findIndex(line => line.includes('CONTENT'));
        const footerIndex = lines.findIndex(line => line.includes('View') && line.includes('Quit'));

        expect(headerIndex).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
        expect(contentIndex).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
        expect(footerIndex).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
        expect(headerIndex).toBeLessThan(contentIndex);
        expect(contentIndex).toBeLessThan(footerIndex);
    });

    it('app shell constrains height to rows prop', async () => {
        const { AppShell, boxCalls } = await loadInstrumentedAppShell();

        render(
            <AppShell columns={80} rows={24} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        expect(boxCalls).toHaveLength(2);
        expect(boxCalls[0]).toMatchObject({ flexDirection: 'column', height: 24 });
    });

    it('content area gets remaining height after header and footer', async () => {
        const columns = 80;
        const rows = 30;
        const expectedFooterLines = computeFooterLines(
            getFooterShortcuts(ViewType.Home),
            columns,
        );
        const { AppShell, boxCalls } = await loadInstrumentedAppShell();

        render(
            <AppShell columns={columns} rows={rows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        expect(boxCalls).toHaveLength(2);
        expect(boxCalls[1]).toMatchObject({
            flexDirection: 'column',
            flexGrow: 1,
            height: rows - HEADER_LINES - expectedFooterLines,
        });
    });

    it('resize updates dimensions', async () => {
        const initialColumns = 120;
        const initialRows = 24;
        const nextColumns = 40;
        const nextRows = 30;
        const { AppShell, boxCalls } = await loadInstrumentedAppShell();
        const { rerender } = render(
            <AppShell columns={initialColumns} rows={initialRows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        rerender(
            <AppShell columns={nextColumns} rows={nextRows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        expect(boxCalls.at(-2)).toMatchObject({ flexDirection: 'column', height: nextRows });
        expect(boxCalls.at(-1)).toMatchObject({
            flexDirection: 'column',
            flexGrow: 1,
            height: nextRows - HEADER_LINES - computeFooterLines(
                getFooterShortcuts(ViewType.Home),
                nextColumns,
            ),
        });
    });

    it('setFooterOptions state reaches Footer props', async () => {
        const ChildThatSetsFooterOptions: React.FC<{
            setFooterOptions: (opts: FooterOptions) => void;
        }> = ({ setFooterOptions }) => {
            useEffect(() => {
                setFooterOptions({ inThread: true });
            }, [setFooterOptions]);

            return <Text>THREAD CONTENT</Text>;
        };

        const { lastFrame } = render(
            <RealAppShell
                columns={200}
                rows={24}
                currentView={{ type: ViewType.Detail, inum: 1 }}
            >
                {(setFooterOptions) => (
                    <ChildThatSetsFooterOptions setFooterOptions={setFooterOptions} />
                )}
            </RealAppShell>
        );

        await tick();
        await tick();

        const frame = stripAnsi(lastFrame()!);

        expect(frame).toContain('THREAD CONTENT');
        expect(frame).toContain('Sub-thread');
        expect(frame).toContain('Exit thread');
        expect(frame).not.toContain('Show pane');
    });
});
```

`src/tui/app-shell.tsx`
```tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { Box } from 'ink';
import { type View } from './views.js';
import { Header, HEADER_LINES } from './header.js';
import { Footer, computeFooterLines, getFooterShortcuts } from './footer.js';
import type { FooterOptions } from './footer.js';

export type SetFooterOptions = (opts: FooterOptions) => void;

export interface AppShellProps {
    columns: number;
    rows: number;
    currentView: View;
    activeAgents?: number;
    maxAgents?: number;
    unreadCount?: number;
    threadInfo?: { inThread: boolean };
    children: (setFooterOptions: SetFooterOptions) => React.ReactNode;
}

export const AppShell: React.FC<AppShellProps> = ({
    columns,
    rows,
    currentView,
    activeAgents,
    maxAgents,
    unreadCount,
    threadInfo,
    children,
}) => {
    const [footerOptions, setFooterOptions] = useState<FooterOptions>({});

    const viewType = currentView.type;
    const footerLines = computeFooterLines(
        getFooterShortcuts(viewType, footerOptions),
        columns,
    );
    const contentHeight = rows - HEADER_LINES - footerLines;

    return (
        <Box flexDirection="column" height={rows}>
            <Header
                currentView={currentView}
                columns={columns}
                activeAgents={activeAgents}
                maxAgents={maxAgents}
                unreadCount={unreadCount}
                threadInfo={threadInfo}
            />
            <Box flexDirection="column" flexGrow={1} height={contentHeight}>
                {children(setFooterOptions as SetFooterOptions)}
            </Box>
            <Footer viewType={viewType} {...footerOptions} columns={columns} />
        </Box>
    );
};
```

Design choices:
- `AppShell` passes the raw `useState` setter through the render prop, cast to the narrower `(opts: FooterOptions) => void` type. That preserves stable function identity, which matters for child `useEffect` dependencies.
- The height tests instrument `Box` with mocks because Ink’s rendered text output doesn’t expose layout props directly. The visible-layout tests still use the real component path.
tokens used
83,171
The workspace is read-only here, so I couldn’t write or run these files directly. These are the complete contents.

`src/tui/app-shell.test.tsx`
```tsx
import { describe, it, expect, vi } from 'vitest';
import React, { useEffect } from 'react';
import { render } from 'ink-testing-library';
import { renderToString, Text } from 'ink';
import stripAnsi from 'strip-ansi';
import { AppShell as RealAppShell } from './app-shell.js';
import { HEADER_LINES } from './header.js';
import {
    computeFooterLines,
    getFooterShortcuts,
} from './footer.js';
import type { FooterOptions, FooterProps } from './footer.js';
import { ViewType } from './views.js';

const tick = () => new Promise(resolve => setTimeout(resolve, 0));
const homeView = { type: ViewType.Home as const };

async function loadInstrumentedAppShell() {
    vi.resetModules();

    const boxCalls: Array<Record<string, unknown>> = [];

    vi.doMock('ink', async () => {
        const actual = await vi.importActual<typeof import('ink')>('ink');

        return {
            ...actual,
            Box: ({ children, ...props }: React.PropsWithChildren<Record<string, unknown>>) => {
                boxCalls.push(props);
                return <>{children}</>;
            },
        };
    });

    vi.doMock('./header.js', async () => {
        const { Text } = await vi.importActual<typeof import('ink')>('ink');

        return {
            HEADER_LINES,
            Header: () => <Text>HEADER</Text>,
        };
    });

    vi.doMock('./footer.js', async () => {
        const { Text } = await vi.importActual<typeof import('ink')>('ink');

        return {
            Footer: (props: FooterProps) => (
                <Text>{props.inThread ? 'THREAD FOOTER' : 'FOOTER'}</Text>
            ),
            computeFooterLines,
            getFooterShortcuts,
        };
    });

    const { AppShell } = await import('./app-shell.js');
    return { AppShell, boxCalls };
}

describe('AppShell', () => {
    it('app shell renders header, content, footer vertically', () => {
        const columns = 120;
        const rows = 12;
        const output = stripAnsi(renderToString(
            <RealAppShell columns={columns} rows={rows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>CONTENT</Text>}
            </RealAppShell>,
            { columns },
        ));

        const lines = output.split('\n');
        const headerIndex = lines.findIndex(line => line.includes('Review Agent Orchestrator'));
        const contentIndex = lines.findIndex(line => line.includes('CONTENT'));
        const footerIndex = lines.findIndex(line => line.includes('View') && line.includes('Quit'));

        expect(headerIndex).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
        expect(contentIndex).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
        expect(footerIndex).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
        expect(headerIndex).toBeLessThan(contentIndex);
        expect(contentIndex).toBeLessThan(footerIndex);
    });

    it('app shell constrains height to rows prop', async () => {
        const { AppShell, boxCalls } = await loadInstrumentedAppShell();

        render(
            <AppShell columns={80} rows={24} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        expect(boxCalls).toHaveLength(2);
        expect(boxCalls[0]).toMatchObject({ flexDirection: 'column', height: 24 });
    });

    it('content area gets remaining height after header and footer', async () => {
        const columns = 80;
        const rows = 30;
        const expectedFooterLines = computeFooterLines(
            getFooterShortcuts(ViewType.Home),
            columns,
        );
        const { AppShell, boxCalls } = await loadInstrumentedAppShell();

        render(
            <AppShell columns={columns} rows={rows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        expect(boxCalls).toHaveLength(2);
        expect(boxCalls[1]).toMatchObject({
            flexDirection: 'column',
            flexGrow: 1,
            height: rows - HEADER_LINES - expectedFooterLines,
        });
    });

    it('resize updates dimensions', async () => {
        const initialColumns = 120;
        const initialRows = 24;
        const nextColumns = 40;
        const nextRows = 30;
        const { AppShell, boxCalls } = await loadInstrumentedAppShell();
        const { rerender } = render(
            <AppShell columns={initialColumns} rows={initialRows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        rerender(
            <AppShell columns={nextColumns} rows={nextRows} currentView={homeView}>
                {() => <Text>BODY</Text>}
            </AppShell>
        );

        expect(boxCalls.at(-2)).toMatchObject({ flexDirection: 'column', height: nextRows });
        expect(boxCalls.at(-1)).toMatchObject({
            flexDirection: 'column',
            flexGrow: 1,
            height: nextRows - HEADER_LINES - computeFooterLines(
                getFooterShortcuts(ViewType.Home),
                nextColumns,
            ),
        });
    });

    it('setFooterOptions state reaches Footer props', async () => {
        const ChildThatSetsFooterOptions: React.FC<{
            setFooterOptions: (opts: FooterOptions) => void;
        }> = ({ setFooterOptions }) => {
            useEffect(() => {
                setFooterOptions({ inThread: true });
            }, [setFooterOptions]);

            return <Text>THREAD CONTENT</Text>;
        };

        const { lastFrame } = render(
            <RealAppShell
                columns={200}
                rows={24}
                currentView={{ type: ViewType.Detail, inum: 1 }}
            >
                {(setFooterOptions) => (
                    <ChildThatSetsFooterOptions setFooterOptions={setFooterOptions} />
                )}
            </RealAppShell>
        );

        await tick();
        await tick();

        const frame = stripAnsi(lastFrame()!);

        expect(frame).toContain('THREAD CONTENT');
        expect(frame).toContain('Sub-thread');
        expect(frame).toContain('Exit thread');
        expect(frame).not.toContain('Show pane');
    });
});
```

`src/tui/app-shell.tsx`
```tsx
import React, { useState } from 'react';
import { Box } from 'ink';
import { type View } from './views.js';
import { Header, HEADER_LINES } from './header.js';
import { Footer, computeFooterLines, getFooterShortcuts } from './footer.js';
import type { FooterOptions } from './footer.js';

export type SetFooterOptions = (opts: FooterOptions) => void;

export interface AppShellProps {
    columns: number;
    rows: number;
    currentView: View;
    activeAgents?: number;
    maxAgents?: number;
    unreadCount?: number;
    threadInfo?: { inThread: boolean };
    children: (setFooterOptions: SetFooterOptions) => React.ReactNode;
}

export const AppShell: React.FC<AppShellProps> = ({
    columns,
    rows,
    currentView,
    activeAgents,
    maxAgents,
    unreadCount,
    threadInfo,
    children,
}) => {
    const [footerOptions, setFooterOptions] = useState<FooterOptions>({});

    const viewType = currentView.type;
    const footerLines = computeFooterLines(
        getFooterShortcuts(viewType, footerOptions),
        columns,
    );
    const contentHeight = rows - HEADER_LINES - footerLines;

    return (
        <Box flexDirection="column" height={rows}>
            <Header
                currentView={currentView}
                columns={columns}
                activeAgents={activeAgents}
                maxAgents={maxAgents}
                unreadCount={unreadCount}
                threadInfo={threadInfo}
            />
            <Box flexDirection="column" flexGrow={1} height={contentHeight}>
                {children(setFooterOptions as SetFooterOptions)}
            </Box>
            <Footer viewType={viewType} {...footerOptions} columns={columns} />
        </Box>
    );
};
```

Design choices:
- `AppShell` passes the raw `useState` setter through the render prop, cast to the narrower `(opts: FooterOptions) => void` type. That preserves stable function identity, which matters for child `useEffect` dependencies.
- The height tests instrument `Box` with mocks because Ink’s rendered text output doesn’t expose layout props directly. The visible-layout tests still use the real component path.
Waiting for the debugger to disconnect...
